question.text: Sell positions in commodity futures that are not squared off before expiry may result in:

answer.text: An obligation to give physical delivery or cash settlement depending on the contract's settlement mode
Automatic rollover to the next month
Cancellation of the position by the exchange
Only a penalty payment with no delivery obligation

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Open short positions at expiry result in either physical delivery or cash settlement as per the contract type. Delivery default leads to penalties, but the primary obligation is delivery/settlement. **The Trap:** No automatic rollover. Exchange doesn't cancel positions. The primary obligation is delivery/settlement, not just penalty. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers short position settlement. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Short sellers who can't deliver must either square off before the tender period or face delivery default penalties — which include replacement cost and can be substantial.
